name: F&B weekend-cycle credit (sector-specific ultra-short revolving)
comment: Dry-run evaluation: module acceptance test on a fresh idea. Thu stock purchase -> weekend receipts -> Mon repayment; 4-7 day credit cycles repaid from routed weekend wallet receipts.

pain_severity.basis: Weekend stock crunch assumed acute for small F&B; needs REQ-002 evidence

financial_impact.basis: Bounded: one weekend's stock, not payroll or expansion

workaround_cost.basis: Produce suppliers often extend informal weekly terms; cost moderate

willingness_to_pay.basis: MCA-style per-cycle fees tolerated in sector; unverified locally

payment_volume.basis: Receipts concentrated on weekends -> high routable share of flow

botim_distribution_advantage.basis: Same as OPP-001; F&B assumed well-represented on BOTIM

transaction_data_advantage.basis: Weekend receipts give fast, dense repayment signal if routed

lending_revenue_potential.basis: Short duration + small balances -> low absolute revenue per merchant

credit_risk_visibility.basis: 5-day feedback loop; default visible within a week

competitive_defensibility.basis: Trivially copyable; is a parameterisation of OPP-001, not a moat

ease_of_validation.basis: Same instruments as VE-001; could ride along in its interviews

mvp_feasibility_7wk.basis: Simpler than OPP-001 concierge: smaller limits, faster cycles
